zh.Louvain, Ille-et-Vilaine, Brittany, Fran: 法國布列塔尼易爾-吉維拉盧瓦
zh.lifeSummary: Arnaud Guillaume Guildaume De Marsan於1230年出生在法國布列塔尼易爾-吉維拉盧瓦。他和End至少有1個女兒。他去世於1272年在法國享年42歲。

nameMeanings.name: Guillaume
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French/Haitian Pierre, Jacques, Jean-Pierre, Marcel, Patrice, Yvon, Anite, Antoine, Edrice, Francois, Gesner, Ghislaine.
French, Walloon, Breton, West Indian (mainly Haiti), and Mauritian: from the personal name Guillaume, French form of William .

variants.name: Guillet
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Jean Marc, Philippe, Pierre, Romain, Yvan.
French: from a pet form of the personal name   Guillaume   ‘William’, derived from its short form   Guille   2, and perhaps also of   Guy   1. Compare   Deyette  ,   Diette  , and   Guillette  .
History: This surname is listed in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors.

variants.name: Guillon
meanings: French: from a pet form of the personal name   Guillaume   ‘William’, derived from its short form   Guille   2.
Hispanic (Guillón): possibly of French origin (see above and Villon ).

variants.name: Guillou
meanings: French: from a pet form of the personal name   Guillaume   ‘William’, derived from its short form   Guille   2.

variants.name: Guillemette
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Normand, Armand, Gilles, Jean-Paul, Leonie, Alcide, Anatole, Cecile, Emile, Fernand, Fernande.

Americanized form of French Guillemet: from a derivative of the personal name Guillaume . Altered ending reflects the Canadian and American French practice of sounding the final -t. Compare Guilmette .
French: from Guillemette, a female personal name derived from Guillaume .

variants.name: Guillot
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Emile, Francois, Landry, Serge, Amelie, Andree, Angelle, Antoine, Eugenie, Jacques, Jean Louis, Joffre.
French: from a pet form of the personal name   Guillaume   ‘William’, derived from its short form   Guille   2. Compare   Diotte  ,   Guillotte  , and   Guyot  .

variants.name: William
meanings: English (of Norman origin): from the Middle English personal name William, Willam, Willem, an Old Picard and Norman French form of ancient Germanic Willihelm, composed of the elements wil- ‘will, desire’ + helm- ‘helmet, protection’. This Norman personal name became widely used in England, Scotland and Wales after the Conquest, mainly no doubt in honor of the Conqueror himself. By the 13th century it had already become the most popular personal name in England. A Parisian form of the name Guillaume was also sometimes used (see Gillam ), which in medieval Wales was adopted as Gwilym (see Gwilliam ).
Americanized form (translation into English) of French   Guillaume  .

nameMeanings.name: Guildaume
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French/Haitian Pierre, Jacques, Jean-Pierre, Marcel, Patrice, Yvon, Anite, Antoine, Edrice, Francois, Gesner, Ghislaine.
French, Walloon, Breton, West Indian (mainly Haiti), and Mauritian: from the personal name Guillaume, French form of William .

variants.name: Guillet
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Jean Marc, Philippe, Pierre, Romain, Yvan.
French: from a pet form of the personal name   Guillaume   ‘William’, derived from its short form   Guille   2, and perhaps also of   Guy   1. Compare   Deyette  ,   Diette  , and   Guillette  .
History: This surname is listed in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors.

variants.name: Guillon
meanings: French: from a pet form of the personal name   Guillaume   ‘William’, derived from its short form   Guille   2.
Hispanic (Guillón): possibly of French origin (see above and Villon ).

variants.name: Guillou
meanings: French: from a pet form of the personal name   Guillaume   ‘William’, derived from its short form   Guille   2.

variants.name: Guillemette
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Normand, Armand, Gilles, Jean-Paul, Leonie, Alcide, Anatole, Cecile, Emile, Fernand, Fernande.

Americanized form of French Guillemet: from a derivative of the personal name Guillaume . Altered ending reflects the Canadian and American French practice of sounding the final -t. Compare Guilmette .
French: from Guillemette, a female personal name derived from Guillaume .

variants.name: Guillot
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Emile, Francois, Landry, Serge, Amelie, Andree, Angelle, Antoine, Eugenie, Jacques, Jean Louis, Joffre.
French: from a pet form of the personal name   Guillaume   ‘William’, derived from its short form   Guille   2. Compare   Diotte  ,   Guillotte  , and   Guyot  .

variants.name: William
meanings: English (of Norman origin): from the Middle English personal name William, Willam, Willem, an Old Picard and Norman French form of ancient Germanic Willihelm, composed of the elements wil- ‘will, desire’ + helm- ‘helmet, protection’. This Norman personal name became widely used in England, Scotland and Wales after the Conquest, mainly no doubt in honor of the Conqueror himself. By the 13th century it had already become the most popular personal name in England. A Parisian form of the name Guillaume was also sometimes used (see Gillam ), which in medieval Wales was adopted as Gwilym (see Gwilliam ).
Americanized form (translation into English) of French   Guillaume  .

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Marsan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Jean-Claude, Lucien, Adelard, Andre, Gaetan, Philippe, Pierre, Raoul.

Altered form of French Mersan, itself a habitational name from a place so named in Orne or a variant of Mersant, a habitational name from a place so named in Eure-et-Loir. The surname Mersan is no longer found in France.
French: habitational name from a place so named in Gers.

variants.name: Lapierre
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Marcel, Armand, Andre, Lucien, Normand, Adrien, Alcide, Aristide, Arnaud, Julien, Pierre, Aime.
French, Walloon, and Flemish:

topographic name, with fused French feminine definite article la, for someone who lived on a patch of stony soil or by a large outcrop of rock, from Old French pierre ‘stone, rock’ (compare Pierre 2); or a habitational name from any of various places called Lapierre or La Pierre in France and Belgium (Wallonia), with the same meaning.

nameMeanings.name: Arnaud
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Germanic. See Arnold .

en.lifeSummary: Arnaud Guillaume Guildaume De Marsan was born in 1230, in Louvain, Ille-et-Vilaine, Brittany, France. He had at least 1 daughter with End. He died in 1272, in France, at the age of 42.

fr.lifeSummary: Arnaud Guillaume Guildaume De Marsan est né en 1230, à Louvain, Ille-et-Vilaine, Bretagne, France. Il a eu au moins 1 fille avec End. Il est décédé en 1272, à France, à l’âge de 42 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Arnaud Guillaume Guildaume De Marsan wurde 1230 in Louvain, Ille-et-Vilaine, Bretagne, Frankreich geboren. Er hatte mit End mindestens 1 Tochter. Er starb 1272 im Alter von 42 Jahren in Frankreich.

it.lifeSummary: Arnaud Guillaume Guildaume De Marsan è nato il nel 1230 nel Louvain, Ille-et-Vilaine, Bretagne, Francia. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlia con End. Morì nel 1272, nel Francia, all'età di 42 anni.

ja.Hill, Warwickshire, England: イギリスワーウィクシュアイアヒル
ja.Louvain, Ille-et-Vilaine, Brittany, Fran: フランスブレタグンイレ-エト-ヴィレアインロウベイン
ja.lifeSummary: Arnaud Guillaume Guildaume De Marsanは1230年にフランスブレタグンイレ-エト-ヴィレアインロウベインで生まれた。彼はEndとの間に少なくとも1人の娘をもうけた。彼は1272年にフランスで42歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Arnaud Guillaume Guildaume De Marsan(은)는 1230년 프랑스 브레타그니 이레-트-빌레이인 루바인에서 태어났다. 그는 End(와)과 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1272년 프랑스에서 42세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Arnaud Guillaume Guildaume De Marsan nasceu em 1230, em Louvain, Ille-et-Vilaine, Bretagne, França. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filha com End. Ele faleceu em 1272, em França, com 42 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Arnaud Guillaume Guildaume De Marsan родился в 1230 г. в местности лоувеин, илле-ет-вилеин, бретагн, Франция. У него была по крайней мере 1 дочь от End. Он умер в 1272 г. в местности Франция в возрасте 42 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Arnaud Guillaume Guildaume De Marsan nació en 1230, en Louvain, Ille-et-Vilaine, Bretagne, Francia. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hija con End. Murió en 1272, en Francia, a la edad de 42 años.
